title: Layout Managers, Resize, Scaling, High DPI, and Reflow · Runtime Lens: Configure Debounce
summary: The Canvas has fixed pixels, controls vanish on a small window, pack and grid conflict in one parent, and high-DPI text clips. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the configure debounce increment in responsive desktop layout and resize policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Handle configure events with bounded coalescing and redraw only from committed current dimensions.
Derive cell size and offsets from current available space, grid dimensions, minimum target, and aspect policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. A defensible solution applies Configure Debounce, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to responsive desktop layout and resize policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-layout-resize-scaling-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-layout-resize-scaling-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_layout_resize_scaling_2(available_width: int = 720, available_height: int = 540, grid_extent: int = 18, cell_size: int = 28, visible_controls: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if min(available_width, available_height, grid_extent, cell_size) < 1:
        return False, cell_size, "layout-shape"
    if cell_size * grid_extent > min(available_width, available_height):
        return False, cell_size, "viewport-overflow"
    if visible_controls < 5:
        return False, visible_controls, "control-clipping"
    if grid_extent > 512:
        return False, grid_extent, "resize-budget"
    assert cell_size * grid_extent <= min(available_width, available_height)
    return True, cell_size, "responsive-maze-layout"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Handle configure events with bounded coalescing and redraw only from committed current dimensions.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Configure Debounce model · Theory Configure Debounce, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Configure Debounce precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Configure Debounce model · Theory Configure Debounce, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 2270 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to derive a responsive cell scale while retaining visible controls and valid geometry; resource use must remain below an explicit budget; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Handle configure events with bounded coalescing and redraw only from committed current dimensions.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Configure Debounce model · Theory Configure Debounce, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Every intermediate resize event requires an immediate full redraw.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-configure-debounce-121lcn.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-configure-debounce-121lcn(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_configure_debounce_121lcn\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?min\(available_width, available_height, grid_extent, cell_size\) < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?cell_size \* grid_extent > min\(available_width, available_height\))(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?visible_controls < 5)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?grid_extent > 512)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.
